issues.description: This should be cardio daily minutes not weekly

fix.description: Root cause: the cardio-minutes stepper showed a weekly total ('min / week', e.g. 180). Fix: it now edits and displays minutes PER DAY ('min / day') = weekly total divided by sessions; changes write back the weekly total as sessions x per-day, so the stored field (used by the plan generator and weekly review) stays correct with no schema change. The sessions stepper preserves the per-day value, and the collapsed summary + plan overview also read min/day. Verified live: cardio shows 35 min/day (120/4); bumping +5 stored weekly 140 = 4 x 35.

Root cause: the Daily-activity stepper labels were truncated with CSS (truncate), clipping to 'Daily steps g...', 'Cardio sessi...', 'Cardio minu...'. Fix: removed the truncate on the label and unit lines - they are short and now render fully ('Daily steps goal', 'Cardio sessions', 'Cardio minutes'). Verified live in the activity sheet.

issues.description: We need option to select glass, bottle and the quality in ml adjusted from profile etc

fix.description: Root cause: the water tracker logged in fixed 250 ml glasses with no way to pick a container or adjust the goal. Fix (full-stack, profile-backed per your choice): the tracker is now ml-based with a container selector - Glass 250, Bottle 500, Large 750, plus whatever default you set - driving a one-tap Add N ml. Daily goal and default container live on your profile (new preferred_water_container_ml column + existing target_water_liters) and round-trip server-side, so a new Profile > Hydration control (goal stepper 1-5 L + container picker) drives what the Nutrition tracker shows. Verified live: picking Bottle then Add moved 1.5 to 2.0 L; setting 3 L + Large in Profile persisted (confirmed via the API) and flowed back to the tracker as 2/3 L with Large pre-selected; zero console errors.

issues.description: Don't show this on the first day once onboarding is done

fix.description: Root cause: useCoachIntake gated the 'coach wants to know you better' quiz on visit count only - a fresh signup's second dashboard visit (minutes after onboarding asked similar questions) fired it on day one. Fix: a nyus_dash_first_seen stamp is written on the first-ever dashboard visit and the drip stays silent that entire day; pinned by a new unit test (suite 10/10). Verified live with a control: first-seen=today + 5 visits + unanswered pool = no card (evidence shot); flipping first-seen to yesterday makes the card appear - the day-1 gate is precisely what suppresses it.

issues.description: Don't show this if not exist for first week

fix.description: Root cause: the Yesterday's-rewards chip showed whenever the status fetch settled, even when yesterday minted zero reward rows - a day-1 user opened it to 'Nothing earned yesterday'. Fix: the chip now requires rewards.length > 0 from the already-fetched status; zero-row days surface no chip at all. Verified live: GET /rewards/daily/2026-06-11 returns 0 rows for this account, seen-marker cleared, plan active - and the dashboard renders no chip (evidence shot; old code would have shown the empty reveal).

issues.description: Add a little margin here so that the camera cutout and time is visible on Android device

fix.description: Root cause: the Your-plan-is-ready screen (PlanReadyOverview) passed topInset=false to MobilePage, dropping the page-root class that carries the native top safe-area rule, and its DomainHeroHeader has no safe-area padding of its own - so the title rendered under the Android status bar and camera cutout. Fix: topInset turned back on, matching the sibling builder screens; the global cap-native rule now reserves the status-bar clearance (env safe-area-inset-top with a 34px Android fallback) above the hero. Verified with a simulated native shell: page root computes 34px top padding.

issues.description: We can show the meals cards and tap to open the modal for ingredients to adjust when we swapp ang get the meals, not on the manual edit plan this nicely

fix.description: Root cause: coach-built and swapped meals rendered the full inline per-ingredient stepper editor, so a meal that arrives complete became a wall of steppers. Fix: BuilderMealSlot now has a source flag - coach meals show a compact card (ingredient count + names + Adjust) and tap to open a bottom-sheet modal with the same full editor (steppers, unit toggles, add ingredient, describe, Done); manually built meals keep the inline editor. Per your choice the meal stays a card after editing in the modal. Verified live: coach build-all produced compact cards with zero inline steppers, and opening Adjust + bumping Turkey Breast 125 to 135g wrote through while the meal stayed a card. Evidence: the Adjust modal for Turkey Oats Bowl.

issues.description: Make sure we know which meals it is when we scroll this should have the sticky dinner on top when I scroll past it

fix.description: Root cause: MealSlotCard's header scrolled away with the card, so long ingredient lists had no meal context mid-scroll. Fix: the header row (slot chip + name + actions) is now sticky top-0 within the diet builder's scroll region, with the card surface and top rounding sliding over the rows and a hairline marking the pinned edge. Evidence: scrolled deep into Lunch's ingredients with the Lunch header pinned under the page bar; pinning is also geometry-asserted (header top == scroller top while rows scroll).

issues.description: Don't let user wait here preload all the same calories for breakfast option and let user swap multiple

fix.description: Root cause: every open of the meal-idea swiper fetched a fresh LLM deck - a 6-8 second 'Cooking up ideas' wait per tap, and trying another idea meant waiting again. Fix: session-scoped deck cache keyed by slot + calorie bucket + diet/goal/allergies; the diet builder preloads every slot's deck in the background right after mount, the swiper serves from the cached deck (only 'More ideas' forces a fresh fetch), and accepting a card drains it from the deck so reopening starts on the next fresh idea. Verified live: tap-to-cards 51ms with the loading spinner never appearing (was seconds), accept then reopen 45ms with the deck consumed 6 to 5 and the next idea up first - swap as many times as you like without waiting.

issues.description: Fix this button text and color

fix.description: Root cause: MealSlotCard's describe-meal Add button combined dark:bg-d-pink-500 with disabled:bg-muted - equal CSS specificity, and the dark variant wins on cascade order, so in dark mode the DISABLED button stayed full pink with white text and looked tappable while doing nothing. Fix: explicit dark:disabled:bg-muted + dark:disabled:text-muted-foreground compound variants. Verified at 390x844 dark mode via computed styles: disabled now renders muted gray (rgb 29,40,58) with muted text; typing into the field flips it to pink/white (enabled).

issues.description: The workout is same for all these days it should be unique per day fun and scientific plan this fix

fix.description: Root cause: the algorithmic exercise selector ranks curated>compound>difficulty with rand() only breaking ties, and the 2-worker build-all sent zero sibling context - so every day of the week got the same top-ranked picks (Floor Press + Pike Push-Up on all 9 days). Fix: /ai/suggest-workout-day now accepts exclude_exercise_ids and seeds the selector's dedupe set with them (with a <3-picks fallback so thin pools never starve a day); the builder builds days sequentially, accumulating each built day's ids, and 'More ideas' excludes the whole board for fresh re-suggests. Verified with a live 7-day coach build: 36 exercise slots, zero cross-day duplicates (Day1 Upper: Floor Press/Diamond Push-Up/Chin-Up/Pull-Up/Pike Push-Up/Spider Curl; Day2 Lower: Plank Jack/Frog Pump/Cable Glute Kickback/Glute Bridge/Calf Raise; Day5 Legs: Bulgarian Split Squat/RDL/Leg Curl/Hip Thrust; Day7 Conditioning: Turkish Get-Up/Lunge Jump/Cossack Squat/Woodchop/Bear Crawl/Hanging Knee Raise).
